mnemonics: ON is 1, OFF is 0: Just like a light switch, 1 turns a binary signal ON, 0 turns it OFF.
keyPoints: Binary logic uses two discrete values: 1 (True/High) and 0 (False/Low).
The three fundamental logic operations are AND, OR, and NOT.
Logic gates are the physical hardware that implement Boolean functions.
commonMistakes: Confusing the output of an AND gate (only 1 if ALL inputs are 1) with an OR gate (1 if ANY input is 1).
Forgetting that a NOT gate (inverter) simply reverses or complements its single input.
